title: Rate Limit Info DTO
description: Canonical 429 (Too Many Requests) response body, standardized in BE#670. Composes error_envelope_schema.json (Shape A) and constrains `error_code` to RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED with a rate-limit `details` block. Accompanying response headers are documented in `x-response-headers`: `Retry-After` is always set; `X-RateLimit-Limit` / `X-RateLimit-Remaining` / `X-RateLimit-Reset` are set only when the limiter is configured to include headers.

scope.description: Rate-limit scope the breach applies to (e.g. "global").

retry_after.description: Seconds the client should wait before retrying (mirrors the Retry-After header).

limit.description: Configured limit window, e.g. "100 per minute" (present only when the limiter includes headers).

reset_time.description: When the window resets, ISO-8601 UTC (present only when the limiter includes headers).

Retry-After.description: Seconds to wait before retrying. Always set on a 429.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Configured limit window, e.g. "100 per minute". Set only when the limiter includes headers.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining requests in the window ("0" on a 429). Set only when the limiter includes headers.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix epoch seconds when the window resets. Set only when the limiter includes headers.
